findings.evidence: Lines 52-55 prove `of_tendsto` from `∀ᶠ i in l, IsPositiveDefinite (F i)`, so at `l = atTop` the natural sequential signature allows finitely many non-positive-definite initial terms.
findings.fix: Change the sequential specialization to take `hF : ∀ᶠ n in atTop, IsPositiveDefinite (F n)` and prove it by `of_tendsto hF hlim`; keep the current forall version only as an additional corollary if desired.
findings.issue: `of_seq_tendsto` requires `hF : ∀ n, IsPositiveDefinite (F n)`, although the theorem it specializes only needs eventual positive-definiteness along the filter.

summary: The filter-level theorem is stated at the right level, but the sequential convenience theorem unnecessarily strengthens the positive-definiteness hypothesis and loses the finite-initial-segment use case that the main theorem already supports.
